heading: Accessibility of certain flight data
label: 47124a Accessibility of certain flight data
notes: References in TextSection 2 of the Pilot’s Bill of Rights, referred to in subsec. (a)(5), is , which is set out as a note under .section 2 of Pub. L. 112–153section 44703 of this titleThe date of enactment of the Fairness for Pilots Act, referred to in subsec. (c), is the date of enactment of subtitle C of title III of div. B of , which was approved .Pub. L. 115–254Oct. 5, 2018

children.heading: .—Administration
children.label: 1 .—Administration
children.text: The term “Administration” means the Federal Aviation Administration.

children.heading: .—Administrator
children.label: 2 .—Administrator
children.text: The term “Administrator” means the Administrator of the Federal Aviation Administration.

children.heading: .—Applicable individual
children.label: 3 .—Applicable individual
children.text: The term “applicable individual” means an individual who is the subject of an investigation initiated by the Administrator related to a covered flight record.

children.heading: .—Contract tower
children.label: 4 .—Contract tower
children.text: The term “contract tower” means an air traffic control tower providing air traffic control services pursuant to a contract with the Administration under section 47124.

children.heading: .—Covered flight record
children.label: 5 .—Covered flight record
children.text: The term “covered flight record” means any air traffic data (as defined in section 2(b)(4)(B) of the Pilot’s Bill of Rights ( note)), created, maintained, or controlled by any program of the Administration, including any program of the Administration carried out by employees or contractors of the Administration, such as contract towers, flight service stations, and controller training programs.49 U.S.C. 44703

children.heading: Provision of Covered Flight Record to Administration.—
children.label: b Provision of Covered Flight Record to Administration.—

children.heading: .—Requests
children.label: 1 .—Requests
children.text: Whenever the Administration receives a written request for a covered flight record from an applicable individual and the covered flight record is not in the possession of the Administration, the Administrator shall request the covered flight record from the contract tower or other contractor of the Administration in possession of the covered flight record.

children.heading: .—Provision of records
children.label: 2 .—Provision of records
children.text: Any covered flight record created, maintained, or controlled by a contract tower or another contractor of the Administration that maintains covered flight records shall be provided to the Administration if the Administration requests the record pursuant to paragraph (1).

children.heading: .—Notice of proposed certificate action
children.label: 3 .—Notice of proposed certificate action
children.text: If the Administrator has issued, or subsequently issues, a Notice of Proposed Certificate Action relying on evidence contained in the covered flight record and the individual who is the subject of an investigation has requested the record, the Administrator shall promptly produce the record and extend the time the individual has to respond to the Notice of Proposed Certificate Action until the covered flight record is provided.

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: 1 .—In general
children.text: Not later than 180 days after the date of enactment of the Fairness for Pilots Act, the Administrator shall promulgate regulations or guidance to ensure compliance with this section.

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: A .—In general
children.text: Compliance with this section by a contract tower or other contractor of the Administration that maintains covered flight records shall be included as a material term in any contract between the Administration and the contract tower or contractor entered into or renewed on or after the date of enactment of the Fairness for Pilots Act.

children.heading: .—Nonapplicability
children.label: B .—Nonapplicability
children.text: Subparagraph (A) shall not apply to any contract or agreement in effect on the date of enactment of the Fairness for Pilots Act unless the contract or agreement is renegotiated, renewed, or modified after that date.
